title: Surface Area of Pyramids
why: A pyramid's surface is one base plus several triangle faces. So you need the area of a rectangle/square for the base, the area of a triangle (½ × base × height) for each side, and the skill to add several areas. Warm those up first.
skills: Area of a triangle (½ × b × h)

diagnostic.q: Area of a square that is 4 on each side?
Area of a triangle with base 6 and height 4: ½ × 6 × 4?
A square pyramid has 1 square base and how many triangle faces?

learn.heading: Base area + triangle faces
learn.intro: A square pyramid has 1 square base and 4 triangle faces. Find the base area (side × side), find each triangle's area (½ × base × height), then add the base and all the triangles. Answer is in square units.
examples: Triangle area: ½ × 6 × 4 → first 6 × 4 = 24, then half of 24 = 12.
Add it up: base 16 plus four triangles of 12 each = 16 + 12 + 12 + 12 + 12 = 64 square units.

tiers.intro: Find a triangle's area one step at a time.

items.q: B. Now take half: half of 24 = ___
C. So the triangle area ½ × 4 × 6 = ___
A. Area of a square base, side 5 (5 × 5) = ___
C. Add the base and 4 triangles: 16 + 10 + 10 + 10 + 10 = ___
A. A square pyramid: base 6 × 6, four triangles each ½ × 6 × 5. What is the total surface area?
items.hint: Base = 36. Each triangle = ½ × 6 × 5 = 15, four of them = 60. Total 36 + 60 = 96.

items.q: B. Four triangle faces each have area 9. What is their combined area? (square units)

exit.q: 2. A square pyramid is made of which faces?
exit.hint: A square pyramid has 1 square base and 4 triangular sides.
